MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Dec-15-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Adam S. Donaton, Esq., of WALSWORTH FRANKLIN BEVINS & McCALL, LLP (415-781-7072), appearing for defendant / moving party Dowman Products, Inc.
Oren P. Noah, Esq. of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Paul R. Barnes

RULING:
DEFENDANT DOWMAN PRODUCTS, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Dowman Products, Inc.'s Motion For Summary Judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is granted as to the sole issue of punitive damages. Considered together, Mr. Paul Barnes' deposition testimony, Mr. Lewis'deposition and trial testimony, Mr. Cook's declaration, Mr. Cook's declaration, Mr. Wellington's deposition and trial testimony and Mr. Dowds' deposition testimony create a triable issue whether Mr. Barnes was exposed to asbestos-containing FixAll manufactured by defendant. Defendant satisfied its burden of production and plaintiffs did not present clear and convincing evidence creating a triable issue whether defendant acted with malice, fraud or oppression necessary to warrant an award of punitive damages. All evidentiary objections are overruled. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 = (220/HEK)
